section_title: Refusing oath or affirmation when duly required by public servant to make it.
chapter_title: Of Contempts Of The Lawful Authority Of Public Servants
text:
Whoever refuses to bind himself by an oath or affirmation to state the truth, when required so to bind himself by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall so bind himself, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both. 
 BNSS Classification 
 Simple imprisonment for 6 months, or fine of 5,000 rupees, or both. 
 Non-cognizable 
 Bailable 
 Triable by The Court in which the offence is committed, subject to the provisions of Chapter XXVIII ; or, if not committed, in a Court, any Magistrate.
